long_stay: Higher education (Bachelor's, Master's, PhD programs at a recognized institution)
Recognized preparatory/foundation year for higher education
Secondary education (in specific student exchange programs)
qualifying_courses.notes: Students from the People’s Republic of China must obtain an academic evaluation certificate issued by the Academic Assessment Centre (APS) before being authorised to enrol in a higher education institution.

types.name: Short-Stay Visa
types.notes: For academic programmes of less than 90 days.

types.name: Long-Stay Visa
types.notes: For academic programmes of more than 90 days.

booking_methods.description: Register and fill out your application online.
External service provider for physical appointments, depending on your country.
External service provider for physical appointments, depending on your country.
Direct appointment booking via email or online portal in some regions.

steps.title: Obtain Admission
steps.description: Receive an official letter of acceptance from a recognized Belgian institution.

steps.title: Pay Administrative Fee
steps.description: Most students must pay a fee to the Belgian Immigration Office. €251 for public higher education institutions, €242 for private (as of 2026). Scholarship holders are exempt.

steps.title: Complete Visa Application Form
steps.description: Fill out the visa application form through Belgium’s Visa On Web system.

steps.title: Gather Documents
steps.description: Prepare all required documents according to the checklist.

steps.title: Submit Application and Biometrics
steps.description: Visit the embassy or visa application center for biometrics (fingerprints and photo) and document submission.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 12 months when arriving in Belgium.

required_documents.name: Completed Visa Application Form
required_documents.details: Printed and signed from the Visa On Web portal.

required_documents.name: Admission Letter / Proof of Enrollment
required_documents.details: Official letter of acceptance or proof of registration from a recognized Belgian institution.

required_documents.name: Proof of Sufficient Funds
required_documents.details: Evidence you can support yourself financially during your stay.

required_documents.name: Medical Certificate
required_documents.details: From an embassy-approved doctor, stating absence of specific infectious diseases.

required_documents.name: Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: Certificate of Good Conduct, issued by the competent authority of the country of origin or last residence, not older than 6 months at time of application, legalized or apostilled.
required_documents.notes: Required for applicants aged 18 and over.

required_documents.name: Administrative Fee Receipt
required_documents.details: Proof of payment of the administrative contribution fee.

required_documents.name: Higher Secondary Education Diploma
required_documents.details: Original and certified copy of your higher secondary education diploma or equivalent.

required_documents.name: Motivation Letter
required_documents.details: Supporting letter explaining your choice of studies.

financial_requirements.reference_basis: Set by Royal Decree and indexed annually by the Belgian Immigration Office (IBZ)
acceptable_proof: Blocked Account (transferring total annual amount to a Belgian bank or university, paid back monthly)
Guarantor (Annex 32) with sufficient income (€3,235.88 net/month as of 2026) who signs a formal obligation
exemptions: Scholarship holders from qualifying bodies (for administrative fee)

residence_permit.notes: Register address at the local Commune/Municipality. Police will visit to verify residence. You will receive an A-Card (electronic residence permit) which serves as your ID and allows travel within the Schengen Area.

work_while_studying.notes: Your residence permit (A-Card) will typically mention 'Labor Market: Limited', meaning you do not need a separate work permit for these hours. As of January 2020, international students no longer need a separate Type C work permit.

required_documents: Proof of sufficient academic progress (e.g., 45 credits over two years for Bachelor's/graduate students, 60 credits over two years for Master's students)

post_study_work.process_authority: Local authority of residence (for orientation year application), Employer (for Single Permit application)
requirements: Hold a Bachelor's, Master's, or PhD degree from a recognized Belgian higher education institution
Proof of sufficient funds (€1,062 net/month for 2026-2027)
Apply to the local authority of your place of residence at least 15 days before your student A-card expires
post_study_work.notes: During the orientation year (Search Year), you have unrestricted access to the Belgian labor market and can work full-time. Once you find a job meeting the minimum salary threshold, your employer will apply for a Single Permit (combined residence and work permit).

official_sources.name: Belgian Immigration Office (IBZ)
official_sources.description: The primary legal source for immigration regulations in Belgium.

official_sources.name: FPS Foreign Affairs
official_sources.description: Information on embassy locations and visa types.

official_sources.name: Study in Flanders
official_sources.description: Official regional portal for education in Flanders.

official_sources.name: Study in Wallonia
official_sources.description: Official regional portal for education in Wallonia.

official_sources.name: EU Immigration Portal - Belgium
official_sources.description: General information on immigration to Belgium.

application_forms.name: Annexes for Residence Permit
application_forms.description: Available under Documentation, then Legislation (in French and Dutch only).

tips.title: Request Physical Admission Letter
tips.body: Embassies often reject printed emails as 'originals'. Always request a physical, stamped admission letter if possible.

tips.title: Verify Guarantor Income
tips.body: Ensure your sponsor’s income is well above the required threshold (€3,235.88 net/month as of 2026) after their own taxes and debts.

tips.title: Start Criminal Record Checks Early
tips.body: Criminal record certificates can take weeks to obtain and legalize (Apostille). Start this process early.

tips.title: Ensure Document Translation Accuracy
tips.body: Documents not in English, French, Dutch, or German must be translated by a sworn translator and then legalized as a separate document.

tips.title: Track Academic Progress
tips.body: Track your academic progress each year to meet credit requirements (e.g., 45 credits over two years for Bachelor's/graduate, 60 credits over two years for Master's) and avoid rejection at residence permit renewal.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too late for the visa
common_mistakes.consequence: Visa may not be processed before your intended start date, delaying or preventing your studies.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply at least 3 months before your intended start date.

common_mistakes.consequence: Your visa application will be rejected or significantly delayed.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Check the full list of required documents on your embassy’s website before applying and ensure all are provided.

common_mistakes.consequence: Documents not in an official language (English, French, Dutch, German) will be rejected.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure all non-official language documents are translated by a sworn translator and legalized.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not registering at the municipality on arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: Failure to register within the legal deadline can lead to legal issues with your residence status.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register at the local Commune/Municipality within 8 days of arrival in Belgium.

common_mistakes.mistake: Forgetting to renew the residence permit
common_mistakes.consequence: Your residence status will expire, leading to potential overstay issues.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for renewal at least 15 days before your A-card expires.

common_mistakes.consequence: Your residence permit renewal application can be rejected.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure you meet the required academic progress (e.g., 45 credits over two years for Bachelor's/graduate, 60 credits over two years for Master's).

common_mistakes.mistake: Working more than 20 hours per week during term time
common_mistakes.consequence: Violation of visa conditions, potentially jeopardizing your student visa status.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Stay strictly within the legal limit of 20 hours per week during the academic term.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the Search Year (Orientation Year) deadline
common_mistakes.consequence: You may lose the opportunity to extend your stay to look for work or start a business.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply for the Search Year before your student A-card expires.
